items.title: What history tells us about Arsenal's title chances
items.summary: With Arsenal still four points clear despite a wobble, how confident can they be of winning the title based on Premier League precedent?

items.title: Deijl debuteert prima: 'Maar we moeten niet doen alsof Zidane in De Kuip was'
items.summary: Feyenoord-watcher Martijn Krabbendam en Freek Jansen hebben het over het debuut van Mats Deijl bij Feyenoord. De rechtsback beleefde een prima debuut, maar volgens Krabbendam moet het niet overdreven worden.

items.title: FC Den Bosch en de play-offrace: 'Zal tot het laatst spannend blijven'
items.summary: FC Den Bosch wil zich dolgraag opnieuw plaatsen voor de play-offs, maar waar de Brabanders vorig seizoen na de eerste periode al zeker waren van deelname aan het toetje zullen ze dit seizoen alle zeilen bij moeten zetten. Trainer Ulrich Landvreugd zag zijn ploeg tegen FC Dordrecht dure punten morsen door de 3-2 nederlaag.

items.title: Scotland 'absolutely ready' for World Cup - if no issues with flights and visas
items.summary: Scotland are "absolutely ready to go" at the T20 World Cup after their last-minute invite - provided there are no hiccups with flights and visas ahead of their planned travel to India.

items.title: PSV zet veldenhegemonie van Feyenoord onder druk
items.summary: Feyenoord won al elf keer op rij de VVCS Veldencompetitie en moet dit seizoen de strijd aangaan met PSV. Dit blijkt uit een tussenstand die is gepubliceerd door de Vereniging voor Contractspelers.

items.title: Feyenoord-talent Krediet (16) maakt overstap naar Juventus
items.summary: Feyenoord-jeugdspeler Fortunato Krediet vervolgt zijn loopbaan bij Juventus. De zestienjarige middenvelder heeft in Turijn tot medio 2028 getekend.

items.title: Scorende Dzeko pakt record bij Schalke-debuut: 'Klasse is net goede wijn'
items.summary: Edin Dzeko deed zondag meteen waarvoor Schalke 04 hem deze winter aantrok : scoren. De 39-jarige spits was bij zijn debuut trefzeker en vestigde daarmee een record. De Bosnische goalgetter bewees dat hij het scoren nog niet is verleerd, nu hij de veertig nadert.

items.title: Arteta challenges 'poor' Arsenal after Man Utd loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has challenged Arsenal to show the mental strength to win the Premier League after falling to a nervous 3-2 home defeat against Manchester United.

items.title: Source: Gotham signs FSU star Jordynn Dudley
items.summary: American forward Jordynn Dudley, a two-time NCAA champion with Florida State University, has signed for Gotham FC, a source with knowledge of the deal confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Oviedo coach: Yamal goal from 'another galaxy'
items.summary: Real Oviedo coach Guillermo Almada branded Lamine Yamal a player from "another galaxy" after the Barcelona forward's wonder goal in Sunday's LaLiga meeting between the two sides.

items.title: Serie A’s ‘mega tie-breaker’ leaves Inter clear but Juve catch the eye again | Nicky Bandini
items.summary: Inter and Juventus exchanged remarkable home victories, while dominant Roma were held to a draw at home by Milan Lady Gaga’s electropop banger Born This Way is 15 years old, but by the standards of some Serie A stadium DJs that makes it positively modern. Thumping out at the Olimpico on Sunday night it somehow felt timely. Roma were on the right track, baby, after a first half in which they thoroughly dominated Milan. The expected goals counter had them up by 1.8 to 0.02. Sure, the actual scoreboard still showed 0-0, but the way Donyell Malen kept making space for himself inside the box was enough to convince fans his swaggering debut against Torino seven days before could be no flash in the pan. Continue reading...

items.title: Heading 'likely' contributed to brain injury behind McQueen death, coroner finds
items.summary: Heading a football "likely" contributed to the brain injury which was a factor in the death of former Scotland defender Gordon McQueen, a coroner has found.

items.title: Lucas Vásquez leads way for old-timers to give Leverkusen lift-off in 2026 | Andy Brassell
items.summary: Team with an average age of just under 29 finally ended their losing streak since turn of the year The biggest cheer of the afternoon at the BayArena was a result from elsewhere. A stunning first league defeat of the season for Bayern Munich at home to Augsburg was not important in any sort of title race context – any faint thoughts of that disappeared a while back, and not just for Bayer Leverkusen. No, the shock result of the afternoon, the week, the month, the season means Xabi Alonso’s 2024 double winners remain the only unbeaten Bundesliga champions. “You’re welcome,” said Augsburg’s English language X account, quote-tweeting Leverkusen’s acknowledgment that their record would endure for at least a season more. And if ever Die Werkself needed a lift, it was here and now. They may have stopped the rot with a single-goal win over Werder Bremen, following a run of four losses in their previous six Bundesliga matches, but it was as bare minimum as the scoreline suggested. Against similarly out-of-form opponents (more so, in fact – Bremen last won on 7 November, nine games ago), they crawled to three points. “We are very relieved,” admitted Kasper Hjulmand with laudable candour. Continue reading...

items.title: Ødegaard admits Arsenal title tilt fuelled by pain of three runners-up finishes
items.summary: Captain says it is a ‘good factor’ in Premier League push Arsenal ‘not good enough’ against Manchester United Martin Ødegaard says that Arsenal’s near misses in the Premier League over the past three seasons are on the players’ minds – but only in a way to motivate them to go all the way this time. The captain sought to push a line that Mikel Arteta has also mentioned – the need to stay in the moment and blot out the external noise – as he reflected on Sunday’s 3-2 home defeat by Manchester United. However, the Norwegian midfielder did admit that his club’s run of three successive runners-up finishes was a part of the picture. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Stop the doubt!' From seeing ghosts to a Super Bowl fairy tale
items.summary: "Let me say it one more time. Stop. The. Doubt," exclaimed Seattle Seahawks tight AJ Barner, who, of course, was referring to his leader Sam Darnold.

items.title: Fulham close to agreement for Man City winger Bobb
items.summary: Fulham are close to reaching an agreement to sign winger Oscar Bobb from fellow Premier League side Manchester City.

items.title: Does anyone want to win the Premier League? A 12-point gap is nothing in this crazy season
items.summary: A Man United title triumph is an incredibly unlikely scenario, but unless Arsenal, Man City or Aston Villa start performing like champions, this season could have another improbable twist.

items.title: Beating Arsenal puts Man United into Premier League title race
items.summary: Man United are 12 points behind league-leading Arsenal, but after beating the Gunners on Sunday, can the Red Devils pull off an unlikely title push?

items.title: 'He needs time' - he got four hours. What went wrong for Foxes' Cifuentes?
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at how Marti Cifuentes' time in charge of Leicester unravelled within hours of owner Khun Aiyawatt 'Top' Srivaddhanaprabha appearing to back the manager.

items.title: Heading ball ‘likely’ contributed to Gordon McQueen’s brain injury, coroner finds
items.summary: Former Scotland defender, who played for Manchester United and Leeds during a 16-year career, died at his home in 2023, aged 70 Heading a football “likely” contributed to the brain injury which was a factor in the death of the former Scotland defender Gordon McQueen, a coroner has found. McQueen – who was capped for Scotland 30 times between 1974 and 1981, and played for both Manchester United and Leeds during a 16-year career – died at his home in North Yorkshire in June 2023, aged 70. Continue reading...
